# Basecamp Theme Agent Guide
- **Stack** WordPress parent theme lives in [wp-content/themes/basecamp](wp-content/themes/basecamp); [wp-content/themes/basecamp/functions.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/functions.php) bootstraps all feature modules and is the place to wire new services.
- **Bootstrap** Keep module `require_once` calls grouped (frontend → admin → SEO → REST → cron → dev → ecommerce); comment toggles are the accepted way to disable subsystems.
- **Namespaces** All `inc/` classes live under the `Basecamp\*` namespace hierarchy (`Basecamp\Admin`, `Basecamp\Frontend`, `Basecamp\SEO`, `Basecamp\Core`, `Basecamp\ThemeFunctions`, `Basecamp\Ecommerce`, `Basecamp\Development`). New classes must declare `namespace Basecamp\<Area>;` at the top and be wired with a `require_once` in `functions.php`. Back-compat aliases (`Basecamp_Frontend`, `Basecamp_Settings`) are registered in `functions.php` via `class_alias()` — do not add new ones without a clear compat reason. Global functions used in templates (e.g. `basecamp_get_link_list()`) must be declared in `functions.php`, not inside a namespaced file.
- **Templates** Page and post views live beside the root template files ([wp-content/themes/basecamp/page-home.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/page-home.php), [wp-content/themes/basecamp/template-parts](wp-content/themes/basecamp/template-parts)); prefer adding new layouts here rather than inside `inc/`.
- **Frontend** [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/frontend/class-basecamp-frontend.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/frontend/class-basecamp-frontend.php) (`Basecamp\Frontend\Frontend`, aliased as `Basecamp_Frontend`) runs output buffering to strip self-closing tags, injects menu classes, and offers helpers like `output_critical_css`, `page_navi`, and `related_posts`—call them instead of re-implementing pagination or related content.
- **SVG Icons** The centralized registry in [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/frontend/class-basecamp-svg-icons.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/frontend/class-basecamp-svg-icons.php) (`Basecamp\Frontend\SVGIcons`) feeds both UI and social icons; extend its maps when adding new social providers.
- **Bloat Removal** [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/frontend/remove-bloat.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/frontend/remove-bloat.php) (`Basecamp\Frontend\RemoveBloat`) aggressively dequeues core assets, disables heartbeat, and strips REST/oEmbed links—new code must not rely on those hooks being present on the frontend.
- **SEO Titles** [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/seo/basecamp-title-functions.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/seo/basecamp-title-functions.php) uses extension classes with `maybe_title`; register new contexts by appending FQCNs to `Basecamp\SEO\TitleManager::$extensions` so fallbacks stay consistent.
- **Meta Descriptions** [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/seo/basecamp-meta-description-functions.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/seo/basecamp-meta-description-functions.php) auto-builds descriptions per template; reuse `Basecamp\SEO\MetaDescription::get_meta_description` when custom markup needs the same logic.
- **Social Meta** [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/seo/basecamp-social-meta-functions.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/seo/basecamp-social-meta-functions.php) injects Open Graph/Twitter tags and removes legacy providers; ensure new share images honor the default stored in the `basecamp_default_share_image` theme mod.
- **Theme Settings** [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/admin/class-basecamp-settings.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/admin/class-basecamp-settings.php) (`Basecamp\Admin\Settings`, aliased as `Basecamp_Settings`) is loaded before all other modules—use `Basecamp\Admin\Settings::get('key', 'default')` to read any feature flag or user-configured value.
- **Media/WebP** The filters in [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/img-optimization/basecamp-webp-functions.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/img-optimization/basecamp-webp-functions.php) swap image URLs to `.webp` variants when present; respect `basecamp_should_exclude_webp()` and add the `no-webp` class if markup must keep original assets.
- **Admin UX** [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/admin/class-basecamp-admin.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/admin/class-basecamp-admin.php) (`Basecamp\Admin\Admin`) enforces classic editor, hides menus, brands login, and raises timeouts; new admin features belong inside this class to keep hooks centralized.
- **Admin Helpers** [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/admin/basecamp-admin-helpers.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/admin/basecamp-admin-helpers.php) (`Basecamp\Admin\AdminHelpers`) houses sanitizers and upload tweaks; reuse `AdminHelpers::sanitize_choices()` and `AdminHelpers::sanitize_checkbox()` when registering Customizer or meta controls.
- **Meta Boxes** Two repeaters ship out of the box: [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/theme-functions/basecamp-meta-link-list.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/theme-functions/basecamp-meta-link-list.php) (`Basecamp\ThemeFunctions\MetaLinkList`) for link lists and [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/frontend/class-basecamp-video-carousel-metabox.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/frontend/class-basecamp-video-carousel-metabox.php) (`Basecamp\Frontend\VideoCarouselMetabox`) for video carousels; both expose filters you can extend rather than rolling new storage.
- **REST Endpoints** [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/rest/basecamp-rest-endpoints.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/rest/basecamp-rest-endpoints.php) registers under `basecamp/v1`; follow that pattern (namespaced route + permissive callback) and remove debug `error_log` calls when deploying new endpoints.
- **Scheduled Events** [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/core/basecamp-scheduled-events.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/core/basecamp-scheduled-events.php) (`Basecamp\Core\ScheduledEvents`) is stubbed—schedule recurring jobs here to keep cron wiring isolated from template logic.
- **Development Tools** [wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/development/class-basecamp-development.php](wp-content/themes/basecamp/inc/development/class-basecamp-development.php) (`Basecamp\Development\Development`) loads the DevPilot drawer for local IPs; the class is only instantiated when `REMOTE_ADDR` is `127.0.0.1` or `::1`.
- **Workflows** No build pipeline ships with the theme—flush caches through WP-CLI (`wp transient delete basecamp_critical_css basecamp_critical_css_mtime`) after touching critical CSS and use `wp cache flush` when debugging WebP URL selection.
- **Testing** Functionality is validated manually inside WordPress; verify menu states, SEO tags, and image fallbacks in multiple template contexts, and hit `/wp-json/basecamp/v1/ping` to confirm REST wiring after changes.
- **Extending Tips** Respect existing filters (`basecamp_register_nav_menus`, `basecamp_link_list_meta_box_args`) and prefer feature-specific classes under the correct `Basecamp\*` namespace over dumping logic into templates.
- **Gotchas** The theme removes sidebars (`is_active_sidebar` always false) and blocks speculation/resource hints; if a feature requires them, gate it behind explicit opt-ins. Functions defined inside namespaced files are scoped to that namespace—global template tags must live in `functions.php`.
- **Coding Style** All new classes go under `Basecamp\<Area>` with a matching `namespace` declaration; follow WordPress escaping/sanitizing patterns already present, and keep new hooks self-documenting with short inline comments when behavior is non-obvious.
